rules.label: Use HiveMind During Development
rules.description: Start or resume a HiveMind session for meaningful work and record important decisions, progress, and feedback as structured entries.
rules.evidence_hint: Session start plus concise project-memory entries when work produces durable context.

rules.label: No Freaking Fallbacks
rules.description: Do not add silent fallbacks, auto-recovery chains, or implicit compatibility shims. Prefer explicit failure and explicit configuration.

rules.label: No Implicit Backward Compatibility
rules.description: Do not preserve legacy behavior unless backward compatibility is explicitly required for the task.

rules.label: One Canonical Contract Per Shape
rules.description: Keep one canonical schema or contract per REST or MCP shape. Do not duplicate DTOs, validators, or parallel request or response definitions.
rules.evidence_hint: Reference the canonical schema file when changing public request or response shapes.

rules.label: Preserve MCP/API/Storage Boundaries
rules.description: Keep skrybe-mcp thin, keep storage concerns inside skrybe-api, and do not let fs-jsonl details leak into MCP contracts.
rules.evidence_hint: Reference the files or docs showing the boundary was preserved.

rules.label: Keep It Simple
rules.description: Prefer straightforward implementation over framework cleverness and avoid needless abstraction.

rules.label: Prefer Repo-Portable References
rules.description: Prefer project-relative repo_file references over machine-specific absolute paths whenever a team-safe reference is possible.
rules.evidence_hint: Use repo_file artifacts or links in stored entries when referring to repository files.

rules.label: Honor Idempotency On Creates
rules.description: Session creation, entry append, and rule-check creation must honor Idempotency-Key semantics.
rules.evidence_hint: Reference the test command or contract check that covers create idempotency.

rules.label: Test Relevant Slices
rules.description: Run the most relevant backend or MCP tests before closing work, especially for create flows, contracts, and storage behavior.
rules.evidence_hint: Record the command and result summary for the relevant tests.

rules.label: No Transcript Dumping
rules.description: Store concise structured memory, not raw conversation archives.

rules.label: Respect Git Safety Constraints
rules.description: Do not push and do not create commits unless the human explicitly asks for it.

rules.label: Align Structural Changes With MVP Plan
rules.description: Before widening contracts or making structural changes, align with docs/skrybe-mcp-mvp-plan.md or update the docs first.
rules.evidence_hint: Reference the MVP plan or updated design doc when changing structure or public shape.
